dataset_description: LibriSpeech test.other. Standard harder read-speech ASR benchmark.

rows.reference: I AM FROM THE CUTTER LYING OFF THE COAST
rows.hypothesis: I'm from the cutter lying off the coast.

rows.reference: DON'T CRY HE SAID I WAS OBLIGED TO COME
rows.hypothesis: Don't cry, he said. I was obliged to come.

rows.reference: AND AND YOU HAVE NOT FOUND OUT ANYTHING CAME IN QUICK FRIGHTENED TONES
rows.hypothesis: and you have not found out anything. Came in quick, frightening tones.

rows.reference: I WISH YOU WOULD BELIEVE ME THAT I AM IN AS GREAT TROUBLE ABOUT IT AS YOU ARE
rows.hypothesis: I wish you would believe me that I am in as great trouble about it as you are.

rows.reference: THAT MY FATHER SIR RISDON GRAEME HAS SMUGGLED GOODS HERE
rows.hypothesis: that my father, Sir Risdon Graham, has smuggled goods here.

rows.reference: HE COULD NOT HELP IT HE HATES THE SMUGGLERS YOU SHALL NOT TELL
rows.hypothesis: He could not help it. He hates the smugglers. You shall not tell.

rows.reference: PRAY PRAY SAY YOU WILL NOT ARCHY WAS SILENT
rows.hypothesis: Pray, pray, say you will not. Archie was silent.

rows.reference: THEN AS ARCHY STOOD IN THE DARK LITERALLY AGHAST WITH ASTONISHMENT HE HEARD THE FAINT RUSTLING ONCE MORE AND AGAIN ALL WAS SILENT
rows.hypothesis: Then, as Archie stood in the dark, literally aghast with astonishment, he heard the faint rustling once more, and again all was silent.

rows.reference: HE LAUGHED BUT IT WAS A CURIOUS KIND OF LAUGH FULL OF VEXATION INJURED AMOUR PROPRE AS THE FRENCH CALL OUR LOVE OF OUR OWN DIGNITY OF WHICH ARCHIBALD RAYSTOKE IN THE FULL FLUSH OF HIS YOUNG BELIEF IN HIS IMPORTANCE AS A BRITISH OFFICER HAD A PRETTY GOOD STOCK
rows.hypothesis: He laughed, but it was a curious kind of laugh, full of vexation. —Injured amour proper. —As the prince call our love of our own dignity, of which Archibald Raystroke, in the full flush of his young belief in his importance as a British officer, had a pretty good stock.

rows.reference: IT ALL COMES OF DRESSING UP IN THIS STUPID WAY LIKE A ROUGH FISHER LAD
rows.hypothesis: It all comes of dressing up in this stupid way, like a rough Fisher lad.

rows.reference: COLD WATER CAME ON THIS IDEA DIRECTLY AS HE RECALLED THE FACT THAT THE DARKNESS WAS INTENSE AND CELIA COULD NOT HAVE SEEN HIM
rows.hypothesis: Coldwater came on this idea directly, as he recalled the fact that the darkness was intense, and Celia could not have seen him.

rows.reference: I'LL SOON SHOW THEM THAT I AM NOT GOING TO BE PLAYED WITH
rows.hypothesis: I'll soon show them that I am not going to be played with.

rows.reference: FOR IT SUDDENLY OCCURRED TO HIM THAT HE WAS NOT ONLY A PRISONER BUT A PRISONER IN THE POWER OF A VERY RECKLESS SET OF PEOPLE WHO WOULD STOP AT NOTHING
rows.hypothesis: For it suddenly occurred to him that he was not only a prisoner, but a prisoner in the power of a very reckless set of people who would stop at nothing.

rows.reference: NO HE THOUGHT TO HIMSELF I DON'T BELIEVE THEY WOULD KILL ME BUT THEY WOULD KNOCK ME ABOUT
rows.hypothesis: No, he thought to himself. I don't believe they would kill me, but they would knock me about.

rows.reference: THE KICK HE HAD RECEIVED WAS A FORETASTE OF WHAT HE MIGHT EXPECT AND AFTER A LITTLE CONSIDERATION HE CAME TO THE CONCLUSION THAT HIS DUTY WAS TO ESCAPE AND GET BACK TO THE CUTTER AS QUICKLY AS HE COULD
rows.hypothesis: The kick he had received was a foretaste of what he might expect, and after a little consideration he came to the conclusion that his duty was to escape and get back to the cutter as quickly as he could.

rows.reference: TO DO THIS HE MUST SCHEME LIE HID TILL MORNING THEN MAKE FOR THE NEAREST POINT AND SIGNAL FOR HELP UNLESS A BOAT'S CREW WERE ALREADY SEARCHING FOR HIM HOW TO ESCAPE
rows.hypothesis: To do this he must scheme, lie hid till morning, then make for the nearest point and signal for help, unless a boat's crew were already searching for him. How to escape.

rows.reference: THE WINDOW WAS BARRED BUT HE WENT TO IT AND TRIED THE BARS ONE BY ONE TO FIND THEM ALL SOLIDLY FITTED INTO THE STONE SILL
rows.hypothesis: The window was barred, but he went to it and tried the bars one by one to find them all solidly fitted into the stone sill.

rows.reference: NEXT MOMENT AS HE FELT HIS WAY ABOUT HIS HAND TOUCHED AN OLD FASHIONED MARBLE MANTELPIECE FIREPLACE CHIMNEY
rows.hypothesis: Next moment, as he felt his way about, his hand touched an old-fashioned marble mantelpiece. Fireplace. Chimney.

rows.reference: YES IF OTHER WAYS FAILED HE COULD ESCAPE UP THE CHIMNEY
rows.hypothesis: Yes, if other ways failed he could escape up the chimney.

rows.reference: NO THAT WAS TOO BAD HE COULD NOT DO THAT
rows.hypothesis: No, that was too bad. He cannot do that.

rows.reference: SYMPATHY AND PITY FOR THE DWELLERS IN THE HOZE WERE COMPLETELY GONE NOW AND HE SET HIS TEETH FAST AND MENTALLY CALLED HIMSELF A WEAK IDIOT FOR EVER THINKING ABOUT SUCH PEOPLE
rows.hypothesis: Sympathy and pity for the dwellers in the hose were completely gone now, and he set his teeth fast, and mentally called himself a weak idiot for ever thinking about such people.

rows.reference: A NARROW TABLE AGAINST THE WALL IN TWO PLACES
rows.hypothesis: a narrow table against the wall in two places.

rows.reference: HE WENT AND TRIED TO FORCE HIS HEAD THROUGH RECALLING AS HE DID THAT WHERE A PERSON'S HEAD WOULD GO THE REST OF THE BODY WOULD PASS
rows.hypothesis: He went and tried to force his head through, recalling as he did that where a person's head would go the rest of the body would pass.

rows.reference: BUT THERE WAS NO CHANCE FOR HIS BODY THERE THE HEAD WOULD NOT GO FIRST
rows.hypothesis: but there was no chance for his body there and the head would not go first.

rows.reference: A FELLOW WHO WAS SHUT UP IN PRISON FOR LIFE MIGHT DO IT HE SAID BUT NOT IN A CASE LIKE THIS
rows.hypothesis: A fellow who was shut up in prison for life might do it, he said, but not in a case like this.

rows.reference: SURE YOU'VE LOOKED ROUND EVERYWHERE BOY YES FATHER QUITE
rows.hypothesis: Sure you've looked round everywhere, boy? Yes, father, quite.

rows.reference: I'M GOING HOME TO BREAKFAST
rows.hypothesis: I'm going home to breakfast.

rows.reference: SHALL I COME TOO FATHER NO
rows.hypothesis: Shall I come too father? No.

rows.reference: STOP HERE TILL SIR RISDON COMES DOWN AND TELL HIM I'M VERY SORRY THAT WE SHOULD HAVE CLEARED OUT LAST NIGHT ONLY A BORN FOOL SAW JERRY NANDY'S LOBSTER BOAT COMING INTO THE COVE AND CAME RUNNING TO SAY IT WAS A PARTY FROM THE CUTTER YES FATHER
rows.hypothesis: Stop here till Sir Risdon comes down and tell him I'm very sorry, that we should have cleared out last night. Only a born fool saw Jerry and Andy's lobster boat coming into the cove and came running to say it was a party from the cutter. Yes, Father.

rows.reference: TELL HIM NOT TO BE UNEASY TIS ALL RIGHT AND I'LL HAVE EVERYTHING CLEAR AWAY TO NIGHT
rows.hypothesis: Tell him not to be uneasy. Tis all right, and I'll have everything clear away tonight.

rows.reference: THE DULL SOUND OF DEPARTING STEPS AND A LOW WHISTLING SOUND COMING DOWN THROUGH THE SKYLIGHT WINDOW INTO THE CABIN WHERE ARCHY RAYSTOKE LAY WITH HIS HEAVY EYELIDS PRESSED DOWN BY SLEEP
rows.hypothesis: The dull sound of departing steps and a low whistling sound coming down through the skylight window into the cabin where Archie Raystroke lay with his heavy eyelids pressed down by sleep.

rows.reference: WHAT A QUEER DREAM HE THOUGHT TO HIMSELF
rows.hypothesis: What a queer dream, he thought to himself.

rows.reference: BUT HOW QUEER FOR MISTER GURR TO BE TALKING LIKE THAT TO ANDREW TEAL THE BOY WHO HELPED THE COOK
rows.hypothesis: But how queer for Mr. Gert to be talking like that to Andrew Teale, the boy who helped the cook.

rows.reference: AND WHY DID ANDY CALL MISTER GURR FATHER
rows.hypothesis: And why did Andy call Mr. Gurfather?

rows.reference: THERE WAS AN INTERVAL OF THINKING OVER THIS KNOTTY QUESTION DURING WHICH THE LOW WHISTLING WENT ON
rows.hypothesis: There was an interval of thinking over this naughty question during which the low whistling went on.

rows.reference: AND I'M HUNGRY TOO TIME I WAS UP I SUPPOSE
rows.hypothesis: And I'm hungry too. Time I was up I suppose.

rows.reference: NO HE WAS NOT DREAMING FOR HE WAS LOOKING OUT ON THE SEA OVER WHICH A FAINT MIST HUNG LIKE WREATHS OF SMOKE
rows.hypothesis: No, he was not dreaming, for he was looking out on the sea, over which a faint mist hung like wreaths of smoke.

rows.reference: WHAT DID THEY SAY FALSE ALARM TELL SIR RISDON THEY WOULD CLEAR ALL AWAY TO NIGHT SEE IF ANYTHING HAD BEEN LEFT ABOUT LOBSTER BOAT
rows.hypothesis: What did they say? False alarm. Tell Sir Rizdon they would clear all the way tonight. See if anything had been left about. Lobster boat.

rows.reference: ONCE OUT OF THAT ROOM HE COULD RAN AND BY DAYLIGHT THE SMUGGLERS DARE NOT HUNT HIM DOWN
rows.hypothesis: Once out of that room he could ram, and by daylight the smugglers dare not hunt him down.

rows.reference: OH THOSE BARS HE MENTALLY EXCLAIMED AND HE WAS ADVANCING TOWARD THEM WHEN JUST AS HE DREW NEAR THERE WAS A RUSTLING NOISE UNDER THE WINDOW A COUPLE OF HANDS SEIZED THE BARS THERE WAS A SCRATCHING OF BOOT TOES AGAINST STONE WORK AND RAM'S FACE APPEARED TO GAZE INTO THE ROOM BY INTENTION BUT INTO THE ASTONISHED COUNTENANCE OF THE YOUNG MIDSHIPMAN INSTEAD
rows.hypothesis: Oh, those bars!" he mentally exclaimed. And he was advancing towards them. Well, just as he drew near, there was a rustling noise under the window. A couple of hands seized the bars. There was a scratching of boot-toes against stonework, and Ram's face appeared to gaze into the room by intention, but into the astonished countenance of the young midshipman instead.

rows.reference: RAM WAS THE FIRST TO RECOVER FROM HIS SURPRISE
rows.hypothesis: Ryn was the first to recover from his surprise.

rows.reference: HULLO HE SAID WHO ARE YOU
rows.hypothesis: Hello, he said. Who are you?

rows.reference: GO ROUND AND OPEN THE DOOR I WAS SHUT IN LAST NIGHT BY MISTAKE
rows.hypothesis: Go round and open the door. I was shut in last night by mistake.

rows.reference: I SAW YOU LAST NIGHT AND WONDERED WHOSE BOY YOU WAS
rows.hypothesis: I saw you last night and wondered whose boy he was.

rows.reference: IT WAS YOU FATHER KICKED FOR SHIRKING AND MY WELL I HARDLY KNOWED YOU
rows.hypothesis: It was you, Father, kicked for shirking, and my, well, I hardly knowed you.

rows.reference: NONSENSE
rows.hypothesis: nonsense

rows.reference: WON'T DO SAID RAM GRINNING
rows.hypothesis: Won't do, said Ram Granny.

rows.reference: THINK I DON'T KNOW YOU MISTER ORFICER
rows.hypothesis: Think I don't know you, Mr. Orfasor?

rows.reference: WON'T DO SAID RAM QUICKLY I KNOW YOU
rows.hypothesis: Won't do, said Rem, quickly. I know you.

rows.reference: BEEN PLAYING THE SPY THAT'S WHAT YOU'VE BEEN DOING WHO LOCKED YOU IN
rows.hypothesis: Been playing the Spy, that's what you've been doing. Who locked you in?

rows.reference: ARCHY STEPPED BACK TO THE DOOR LISTENING BUT THERE WAS NOT A SOUND
rows.hypothesis: Archie stepped back to the door listening, but there was not a sound.

rows.reference: HE HAS GONE TO GIVE THE ALARM THOUGHT THE PRISONER AND HE LOOKED EXCITEDLY ROUND FOR A WAY OF ESCAPE
rows.hypothesis: He has gone to give the alarm, thought the prisoner, and he looked excitedly around for a way of escape.

rows.reference: NOTHING BUT THE CHIMNEY PRESENTED ITSELF
rows.hypothesis: Nothing but the chimney presented itself.

rows.reference: A HAPPY INSPIRATION HAD COME AND PLACING ONE HAND UPON HIS BREAST HE THRUST IN THE OTHER GAVE A TUG AND DREW OUT HIS LITTLE CURVED DIRK GLANCED AT THE EDGE RAN TO THE WINDOW AND BEGAN TO CUT AT ONE OF THE BARS LABOUR IN VAIN
rows.hypothesis: A happy inspiration had come, and placing one hand upon his chest, he thrust in the other, gave a tug, and drew out his little curved dirk, glanced at the edge, ran to the window, and began to cut at one of the bars. Labor in vain. CHAPTER IV THE END

rows.reference: HE DIVIDED THE PAINT AND PRODUCED A FEW SQUEAKS AND GRATING SOUNDS AS HE REALISED THAT THE ATTEMPT WAS MADNESS
rows.hypothesis: He divided the paint and produced a few squeaks and grating sounds as he realized that the attempt was madness.

rows.reference: THE RESULT WAS NOT VERY SATISFACTORY BUT SUFFICIENTLY SO TO MAKE HIM ESSAY THE BAR OF THE WINDOW ONCE MORE PRODUCING A GRATING EAR ASSAILING SOUND AS HE FOUND THAT NOW HE DID MAKE A LITTLE IMPRESSION SO LITTLE THOUGH THAT THE PROBABILITY WAS IF HE KEPT ON WORKING WELL FOR TWENTY FOUR HOURS HE WOULD NOT GET THROUGH
rows.hypothesis: The result was not very satisfactory, but sufficiently so to make him essay the bar of the window once more, producing a grating, era-selling sound, as he found that now he did make a little impression, so little though that the probability was, if he kept on working well for twenty-four hours, he would not get through.

rows.reference: BUT AT THE END OF FIVE MINUTES HE STOPPED AND THRUST BACK THE DIRK INTO ITS SHEATH
rows.hypothesis: But at the end of five minutes he stopped and thrust back the dirk into its sheath.

rows.reference: NO I CAN'T PART WITH THAT HA HA HA LAUGHED THE BOY JEERINGLY
rows.hypothesis: No, I can't part with that. Hahaha! Laughed the boy jeeringly.

rows.reference: BUT I'LL YES I'LL GIVE YOU A GUINEA IF YOU WILL LET ME OUT
rows.hypothesis: But I'll... yes, I'll give you a guinea if you will let me out.

rows.reference: GUINEA SAID THE BOY THINK I'D DO IT FOR A GUINEA WELL THEN TWO
rows.hypothesis: Guinea, said the boy, think I'll do it for her guinea. Well then too.

rows.reference: BE QUICK THERE'S A GOOD FELLOW I WANT TO GET AWAY AT ONCE
rows.hypothesis: Be quick, there's a good fellow. I want to get away at once.

rows.reference: NOT YOU ONLY A SHAM
rows.hypothesis: Not you, only a sham.

rows.reference: WHY YOUR CLOTHES DON'T FIT YOU AND YOUR CAP'S PUT ON ALL SKEW REW
rows.hypothesis: Why, your clothes don't fit you and your caps put on all-scuru.

rows.reference: NEVER MIND ABOUT THAT LET ME OUT OF THIS PLACE
rows.hypothesis: Never mind about that. Let me out of this place.

rows.reference: I TOLD YOU A FISHER BOY CRIED ARCHY IMPATIENTLY BUT TRYING NOT TO OFFEND HIS VISITOR WHO POSSESSED THE POWER OF CONFERRING FREEDOM BY SPEAKING SHARPLY
rows.hypothesis: "'I told you a fisher-boy,' cried Archie impatiently, but trying not to offend his visitor, who possessed the power of conferring freedom by speaking sharply."

rows.reference: NOT YOU LOOK LIKE A WILD BEAST IN A CAGE LIKE A MONKEY YOU INSOLENT
rows.hypothesis: Not you. Look like a wild beast in a cage. Like a monkey. You insolent.

rows.reference: ARCHY CHECKED HIMSELF AND THE BOY LAUGHED
rows.hypothesis: Archie checked himself and the boy laughed.

rows.reference: IT WAS YOUR TURN YESTERDAY IT'S MINE TO DAY WHAT A GAME
rows.hypothesis: It was your turn yesterday. It's mine today. What a game.

rows.reference: YOU LAUGHED AND FLEERED AT ME WHEN I WAS ON THE CUTTER'S DECK
rows.hypothesis: You laughed and fleered at me when I was on the cutter's deck.

rows.reference: I SAY YOU DO LOOK A RUM UN JUST LIKE A BIG MONKEY IN A SHOW
rows.hypothesis: I say, you do look like a rumen. Just like a big monkey in a show.

rows.reference: RAM SHOWED HIS WHITE TEETH AS HE BURST OUT WITH A LONG LOW FIT OF LAUGHTER
rows.hypothesis: Ram showed his white teeth as he burst out with a long, low fit of laughter.

rows.reference: YOU ROPE'S END ME HE SAID
rows.hypothesis: You're ropes and me he said

rows.reference: WHY I COULD TIE YOU UP IN A KNOT AND HEAVE YOU OFF THE CLIFF ANY DAY WHAT A GAME
rows.hypothesis: Why, I could tie you up in a knot and heave you off the cliff any day. What a game.

rows.reference: BIT OF A MIDDY FED ON SALT TACK AND WEEVILLY BISCUIT TALK OF GIVING ME ROPE'S END
rows.hypothesis: Bit of a midi fed on a salt tack and weavily biscuit. Talk of giving me ropes and...

rows.reference: ONCE MORE WILL YOU COME AND LET ME OUT NO
rows.hypothesis: Once more, will you come and let me out? No.

rows.reference: TO HIS ASTONISHMENT THE BOY DID NOT FLINCH BUT THRUST HIS OWN ARMS THROUGH PLACING THEM ABOUT THE MIDDY'S WAIST CLENCHING HIS HANDS BEHIND AND UTTERING A SHARP WHISTLE
rows.hypothesis: To his astonishment the board did not flinch, but thrust his own arms through, placing them about the midi's waist, clenching his hand behind, and uttering a sharp whistle.

rows.reference: SEEMED IN GOOD SPIRITS LAST NIGHT MISTER GURR EH
rows.hypothesis: Seemed like good spirits last night, Mr. Gurr, eh?

rows.reference: YES SIR BUT HE MAY TURN UP ON THE CLIFF AT ANY MOMENT
rows.hypothesis: Yes, sir, but he may turn up on the cliff at any moment.

rows.reference: YES MEN QUITE READY YES SIR
rows.hypothesis: Yes, men quite ready? Yes sir.

rows.reference: THAT'S RIGHT OF COURSE WELL ARMED
rows.hypothesis: That's right, of course. Well armed.

rows.reference: SOON AS THE SIGNAL COMES WE SHALL PUSH OFF
rows.hypothesis: As soon as the signal comes we shall push off.

rows.reference: AWKWARD BIT O COUNTRY SIR SIX MILES ROW BEFORE YOU CAN FIND A PLACE TO LAND
rows.hypothesis: Awkward bit of country, sir. Six miles, row, before you can find a place to land.

rows.reference: SO SHALL WE YET SIR
rows.hypothesis: So shall we, yes sir.

rows.reference: YOU DON'T THINK MISTER GURR THAT THEY WOULD DARE TO INJURE HIM IF HE WAS SO UNLUCKY AS TO BE CAUGHT
rows.hypothesis: You don't think, Mr. Gurr, that they would dare to injure him if he was so unlucky as to be caught.

rows.reference: WELL SIR SAID THE MASTER HESITATING SMUGGLERS ARE SMUGGLERS
rows.hypothesis: Well, sir," said the master, hesitating, "'smugglers are smugglers.'"

rows.reference: CERTAINLY SIR SMUGGLERS ARE SMUGGLERS INDEED
rows.hypothesis: Certainly, sir. Smugglers are smugglers indeed.

rows.reference: BEG PARDON SIR DIDN'T MEAN ANY HARM
rows.hypothesis: Beg pardon, sir. Didn't mean any harm.

rows.reference: I'M GETTING VERY ANXIOUS ABOUT MISTER RAYSTOKE START AT ONCE SIR
rows.hypothesis: I'm getting very anxious about Mr. Raystroke. Start at once, sir.

rows.reference: NO WAIT ANOTHER HALF HOUR
rows.hypothesis: No, wait another and a half hour.

rows.reference: VERY ILL ADVISED THING TO DO
rows.hypothesis: A very ill-advised thing to do.

rows.reference: THEN I MUST REQUEST THAT YOU WILL NOT MAKE IT AGAIN VERY TRUE
rows.hypothesis: Then I must request that you will not make it again. Very true.

rows.reference: AWK WARD MISTER GURR AWKWARD
rows.hypothesis: Awkward, Mr. Gurr. Awkward.

rows.reference: YES SIR OF COURSE
rows.hypothesis: Yes, sir, of course.

rows.reference: SAY AWK WARD IN FUTURE NOT AWK'ARD
rows.hypothesis: Say awkward in the future, not awkward.

rows.reference: I MEAN ALL ALONE BY MYSELF SIR
rows.hypothesis: I mean all alone by myself sir.

rows.reference: WHAT FOR THERE AREN'T A PUBLIC HOUSE FOR TEN MILES DIDN'T MEAN THAT
rows.hypothesis: What for? There aren't a public house for ten miles. Didn't mean that.

rows.reference: THEN WHAT DID YOU MEAN SPEAK OUT AND DON'T DO THE DOUBLE SHUFFLE ALL OVER MY CLEAN DECK NO SIR
rows.hypothesis: Then what did you mean? Speak out and don't do the double shuffle all over my clean deck. No, sir.

rows.reference: HOPPING ABOUT LIKE A CAT ON HOT BRICKS
rows.hypothesis: Hopping about like a cat on hot bricks.

rows.reference: NOW THEN WHY DO YOU WANT TO GO ASHORE
rows.hypothesis: Now then, why do you want to go ashore?

rows.reference: BEG PARDON DIDN'T MEAN NOWT SIR SAID THE SAILOR TOUCHING HIS FORELOCK
rows.hypothesis: Beg pardon, didn't mean now, sir, so the sailor touching us for a while.
